pages.full_text:
We started preparing lunch outside & had to eat it in the van. About 3 showers blew across, one of them very heavy, blowing rain horizontally & wetting the chairs under the table. We read books in the van & snoozed. After tea Simon went off for an hour's walk with Peter & Thomas
    We went to bed, Thomas & Peter in the tent. At 2.30 I over heard Thomas & Peter having an argument & finally the van door opened & Peter tottered in, soakily wet, sodden pyjamas & freezing cold. Thomas was wet too.
    Apparently the extra outer ground sheet had collected
pages.route_sketch_description: Small hand-drawn triangle shape at bottom-left of the page, likely a simple sketch of a tent.
pages.notes: Text continues onto right page. The triangle sketch at the bottom-left appears to be a simple tent outline, not a route map.

pages.full_text:
the rainwater off the tent & the sleeping bags had soaked it up as it soaked in [illegible] through the groundsheet.
    The 4 of us tried to sleep shoulder to shoulder, but finally Simon got up & walked & read, leaving the 3 of us to sleep. We should have put T & P with their heads the other end.
    We went to church at Woodford Halse where they had 2 christenings. In the afternoon we saw the big House at Canons Ashby & joined the N.T.. Parts of the house & church feel very old, but [?18] modernisations. Beautiful tapestries & interesting rooms.
pages.notes: Continuation from left page. 'N.T.' = National Trust. The struck-through word before 'through' is partially legible — appears to be 'under' crossed out. The '[?18]' likely refers to 18th century modernisations. No year is explicit on this spread; year cannot be confidently determined from content alone.
